name: Amphotericin B lipid complex
executiveSummary: Febrile neutropenia on day 7. Pulmonary infiltrate during neutropenia. Documented systemic mycoses.
indications:
## Main indications

Febrile neutropenia on day 7. Pulmonary infiltrate during neutropenia. Documented systemic mycoses.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Vial containing 100 mg in 20 mL solution (Abelcet).
adulto.reconstitution: No prior reconstitution required. Conc: 5 mg/mL.
adulto.diluent: 100 mg in 100 mL 5% dextrose. Do not use 0.9% NaCl.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: No. Intermittent IV: Yes. Withdraw required dose with syringe and use filter supplied with vial. Add required dose to 100 mL 5% dextrose and administer over 120 min.
adulto.notes: All unused material must be discarded (contains no preservatives). Use filter needle to withdraw solution from vial and then prepare infusion.

pediatrico.dose: Start 0,5 mg/kg/day, increase 0,2 mg/kg/day every 24 h, up to 1 mg/kg/day, maximum dose: 50 mg/day. Rapid administration method: start 0,25 mg/kg and increase at 6 h intervals up to 1 mg/kg/day. Do not exceed 1,5 mg/kg/day.
pediatrico.notes: Infuse over 4–6 h at concentration of 0,1 mg/mL in 5% dextrose via peripheral line and up to 0,5 mg/mL via central line. Premedicate to reduce adverse reactions with hydrocortisone, paracetamol, diphenhydramine 30 min before infusion. Do not administer with solutions containing electrolytes. Correct hypokalemia before administration.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- Single-use vial. Keep refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- 6 h at room temperature and 48 h refrigerated. Use light-protected infusion set.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Fever, chills, vomiting (infusion-related), hypokalemia (25%), hypomagnesemia. Nephrotoxicity: may be prevented with prior saline infusion. Anemia (75%). Rare: leukopenia, thrombocytopenia.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — Anfotericina B (DEOXICOLATO) (code 0020, ATC J02AA).
Infectious Diseases Service, Infection Prevention and Control. UCIP 2026 — Dilution and stability guide.

ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
